contentBlocks.original:
कर्पूरगौरं करुणावतारं
संसारसारम् भुजगेन्द्रहारम्
सदावसन्तं हृदयारविन्दे
भवं भवानीसहितं नमामि
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karpūragauraṃ karuṇāvatāraṃ
saṃsārasāram bhujagendrahāram
sadāvasantaṃ hṛdayāravinde
bhavaṃ bhavānīsahitaṃ namāmi
contentBlocks.translation: I offer my salutations to Lord Shiva, who is as radiant as camphor, an embodiment of compassion, the essence of the world, adorned with the serpent king as a garland, who always resides in the lotus heart of devotees, along with Goddess Bhavānī (Parvati).
contentBlocks.meanings: कर्पूरगौरं (karpūragauraṃ) – camphor-white (karpūra:camphor | gauraṃ:white); करुणावतारं (karuṇāvatāraṃ) – embodiment of compassion (karuṇā:compassion | avatāraṃ:embodiment); संसारसारम् (saṃsārasāram) – essence of the world (saṃsāra:world | sāram:essence); भुजगेन्द्रहारम् (bhujagendrahāram) – garland of serpent king (bhujaga:serpent | indra:king | hāram:garland); सदावसन्तं (sadāvasantaṃ) – always residing (sadā:always | vasantaṃ:residing); हृदयारविन्दे (hṛdayāravinde) – in the lotus heart (hṛdaya:heart | aravinde:in the lotus); भवं (bhavaṃ) – Lord Shiva; भवानीसहितं (bhavānīsahitaṃ) – with Goddess Bhavānī (bhavānī:Goddess Bhavānī | sahitaṃ:with); नमामि (namāmi) – I offer salutations;
